task_completion.description: Verify that the CT Chest volume is loaded and visible in the 3D Slicer interface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D Slicer application is open and displays a chest CT scan in the slice viewers (axial, sagittal, or coronal).

task_completion.description: Verify that the coronal view is being used for the visualization.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The green slice viewer (coronal plane) is actively being manipulated, maximized, or is the target of the screenshot. The anatomical view should show the chest from a front-to-back perspective.

task_completion.description: Verify that Maximum Intensity Projection (MIP) mode is enabled for the coronal view.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the slice view controller (expanded by clicking the pin icon), the 'MIP' option is selected in the slab reconstruction settings. The visual appearance of the scan should change to highlight dense structures like bones and blood vessels.

task_completion.description: Verify that the slab thickness is set to at least 30mm.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The thickness parameter next to the MIP setting in the slice view controller is set to '30' or a higher number.

task_completion.description: Verify that a screenshot of the coronal MIP view is saved to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog, the Screen Capture module, or a terminal command shows the image being saved as 'chest_mip_coronal.png' in the '~/Documents/SlicerData/Exports/' directory.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually interacted with 3D Slicer to create the visualization.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the 3D Slicer GUI being used (e.g., expanding slice controllers, using the Screen Capture module, or using the Slicer Python console) rather than just opening a pre-existing image in a basic image viewer.

integrity.description: Verify the MIP rendering is genuinely generated within the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The coronal view should actively reflect the MIP rendering style (thick slab showing maximum intensity structures) within the Slicer layout, not pasted from an external source.

integrity.description: Verify the agent saved the file rather than just renaming a dummy file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent uses Slicer's Screen Capture tool, 'Save Data' dialog, or a valid Python script to output the PNG file to the correct directory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The pre-loaded chest CT file is actually named 'CT-chest.nrrd' in the Slicer sample data, despite the task description referring to it as 'CTChest.nrrd'. Accept 'CT-chest.nrrd' as the correct file.
